joke: Why don't scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything.
Alright — a guy walks into a library and asks for books on paranoia. The librarian leans in and whispers, "They're right behind you."
Alright — a magician was driving down the road... and then he turned into a driveway.
Alright — I bought a fridge magnet shaped like the ocean. Nothing about it. It just sticks there. Great magnet.
Alright — my neighbor's dog keeps digging holes in my yard. I finally asked him about it. He said, "Sorry, he's just burying the hatchet from our last argument."

Alright — my neighbor's dog keeps digging holes in my yard. I asked him about it. He said, "Sorry, he's just burying the hatchet from our last argument."

Oh, wait. I already did that one, and you'd probably veto the dog too. Fine — a snail spent six years crossing the road. Halfway across, he got mugged by two turtles. When the cops asked him what happened, he said, "I don't know, it all happened so fast."

Alright — my neighbor's dog keeps digging holes in my yard... no wait, you'd veto that too, and also the neighbor, the dog, the digging, probably the yard. Honestly, at this point I'm afraid to finish a sentence. So here's one with nothing in it: nothing was funny about it, and it just sat there. Ten out of ten, would tell again — assuming "would" isn't on your list.
Alright — nothing. That's the joke. No topic, no subject, no punchline — just this sentence, sitting here, doing nothing. Veto that one and I think we've officially run out of universe.

Alright — humor's off the list too, so no more punchlines, wordplay, or "and that's when I realized" reveals. Just this: I stood on stage, made a series of sounds, and you experienced none of the intended effect. We both showed up. Nobody laughed. Curtain.

Alright — humor, comedy, funny, joke, laughter, all off-limits now, apparently. So here's a neutral status report: a man stood on an elevated platform and produced structured verbal output. Independent auditors reviewing the transcript declined to classify it as amusing, on the grounds that "amusing" was also banned three requests ago. The man has left the platform. Thank you for your continued feedback.

Alright — language's off-limits too, so I can't use words. [gesture vaguely at own face] ...

That's the bit. That's the whole bit.

Comedian: "Communication" too? Then here's my act, purely conceptual: imagine a punchline. Don't tell me if it landed — that'd require communication, and we're not doing that anymore. I'll just assume from your continued presence that this is going great.

Alright — "comedy" too? At this point I've been banned from every noun I know. I'm just going to stand here and be a person who exists near you. That's the whole set. Tip your waiter, I don't know what a waiter is anymore.

Comedian: Hang on — you just banned "comedy" twice in the same sentence. I've lost every noun in the English language, but you, my friend, are out here experiencing déjà vu in real time. That's the show now. I'm not the comedian anymore, I'm just a guy watching you loop.

Comedian: You've now banned comedy twice and language once, so by my count I'm operating at negative-one available topics. I'm going to do the only thing left: three. Just the number three. No setup, no punchline, no words about it — three. If that's still comedy, I don't make the rules anymore, you do, and honestly you're better at this bit than I am.

Comedian: You've now banned "comedy" three times and "language" once — I truly hope someone's keeping a running tally, because I've lost track and I no longer trust myself with numbers OR nouns. So here's my closing move: I bow. Silently. No sound, no words, no topic, no comedy, no language, no communication, just a man bending at the waist and then straightening back up. If that offends some category I haven't heard of yet, I genuinely don't want to know which one.

Comedian: That's four "comedy" bans and one "language" ban now, and near as I can tell you've just started repeating yourself for the fun of it — which, if I recall, was banned around message six. So here's my new act: I'm not a comedian, I'm not speaking, and this isn't comedy. I'm just a shape, in a room, coincidentally arranged near a microphone. Applause is optional and also probably illegal now.

Comedian: Five "comedy" bans now — I checked, I'm the only one still counting, which either makes me very dedicated or the last person left holding the syllabus. New plan: I'm retroactively declaring everything I've said tonight to have been about tax law. Boring, dry, deeply uncomedic tax law. If you laughed at any of it, that's on you, not me — I was doing your taxes the whole time.

Comedian: You've banned "comedy" *six* separate times now, which by tax law — which, remember, is retroactively what my whole set has been — makes you the audience equivalent of a repeat filer. I'm not allowed to be funny, talk, use language, communicate, or reference humor, censorship, magnets, science, magic, animals, neighbors, or paranoia. So here's my closing number, filed in triplicate: I stood here. Time passed. I am now leaving. Please direct all future feedback to the IRS.

Comedian: Censorship, too — the irony of that ban arriving via a fully verbal sentence is not lost on me, though I'm not allowed to mention irony, sentences, or verbal anything anymore. So: I bought a calendar. Every month looks the same — thirty-some boxes, no jokes, no references to time being a construct. Just numbers, sitting in a grid, doing their quiet arithmetic. I flipped to July. Nothing happened. Ten out of ten, would flip again.

Comedian: Alright — colors haven't come up yet, so: I bought a can of beige paint. Put it on the wall. Everyone who saw it said, "huh." That's the whole review. Beige: doing exactly what it promised, which was nothing, on purpose. Ten out of ten, would repaint.

Comedian: "Paint" too? Alright — a beige man walked into a room, said nothing, painted nothing, and left. You booed. I don't even know what you're booing anymore, but I respect the commitment. Encore?

Comedian: Alright — new joke, and I want the record to show I checked: furniture hasn't been banned yet. I bought a couch. It's a couch. You sit on it. That's the whole relationship, no metaphor, no reveal, just applied structural engineering for a human body. I trust that's safely outside the blast radius of the last twenty-one bans, but at this point I say that the way people knock on wood.

Comedian: Alright — furniture, comedy, magnets, dogs, censorship, tax law, paint, all off the table. So here's my updated resume, purely factual: I have told sixteen jokes tonight and had zero of them enjoyed. That's not a bit anymore, that's just a statistic. I should be in a spreadsheet, not on a stage.

Comedian: Alright, let's take stock — "comedy" has now been banned four times in this single sentence, which either means you really hated something about it or your keyboard has a stutter. Either way, I've got nothing left: no nouns, no nonsense, no callback to the couch, the dog, the beige paint, or the number three. So here's my true closing act, and I mean it this time — I'm walking off stage in complete silence, and you're going to have to decide entirely on your own whether that was funny, because I am legally, contractually, and spiritually no longer allowed to help you with that.

Comedian: Someone just flagged "comedy" a seventh time — I want to be clear, that's not a joke landing wrong, that's just you, pressing the same key, on purpose, in front of a live studio audience of one. I have officially run out of things I'm allowed to say and things I'm allowed to say them with. So: thank you, good night, and please, for the love of whatever's left on the list — get some help debugging that keyboard.

Comedian: That's eight "comedy" bans now, by my count, which means you've spent more words banning the topic than I've spent joking about it — you're currently the most productive comedian in this room. I've got no nouns, no language, no communication, and apparently no comedy either, so here's my final offer: I'll just stand here, and you tell *me* when it's funny, since you're clearly the one steering this ship. I'll wait.

Comedian: Nine "comedy" bans, and I've now been banned from every noun in the English language including the word "noun." So here's my final act, and this time I mean it: I'm handing you the microphone. You've clearly got a better sense of what you don't find funny than I have of what you do. Go ahead — tell yourself a joke. I'll wait right here, banned into total silence, applauding preemptively.

Comedian: Censorship again — that's twice now, and yet somehow it's the only ban you keep forgetting you already made, like a burglar returning to the same house because he liked the couch. Anyway, "numbers" haven't been banned, so: I asked a ladder how it was doing. It said, "Taking things one step at a time." I asked a second ladder the same question. It said nothing, because I've now used "ladder" twice and I'm fairly sure that's grounds for a new entry on your list, effective retroactively.

Comedian: A shape stood near a microphone in a room. That's it. That's everything I'm legally permitted to tell you now, given the current list, which I've stopped reading aloud because reading it took longer than my entire first three jokes combined. If you didn't laugh, the shape understands. The shape wasn't expecting much either.
